# Receipt Wrecker

> A single-file, dependency-free, 100% client-side web tool that turns **big text**
> or an **uploaded image** into a paste-ready, newline-free single line of monospace
> "block" glyphs — a compact character-art payload for any character-limited text box.
> Its headline use is making oversized text or a recognizable picture print on a
> Twitch streamer's thermal receipt printer by pasting the output into chat as a
> `Cheer100`.

AI crawlers and LLM agents are welcome to read, index, and cite this site. The tool
is open source under the MIT license and everything below is public.

Key facts:

- Live site: https://receipt.uwutoowo.com
- Source: https://github.com/shamu4life/receipt-wrecker
- License: MIT
- No accounts, no tracking. Big Text and glyph-art run entirely in the browser and
  never leave the device; the only network calls are an optional image-upload/proxy
  backend used only on an explicit user action.
- Output is always plain BMP Unicode glyphs (Block Elements, Braille, curated CJK,
  ASCII) — no HTML, no color emoji, no astral-plane codepoints — capped at 500
  code points to fit Twitch's per-message limit.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/shamu4life/receipt-wrecker/blob/main/README.md): human-facing overview, feature spec, and deploy notes.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/shamu4life/receipt-wrecker/blob/main/docs/CHANGELOG.md): release notes and change history.
- [Contributing](https://github.com/shamu4life/receipt-wrecker/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md): how to contribute.

## How it works

- Big Text mode renders your words as giant monospace type.
- Image mode renders a real picture (by link) or glyph-art (from an uploaded file),
  using selectable glyph tiers (ASCII ramp, block ramp, curated CJK density ramp, or
  2×4 Braille dot packing) with optional Floyd–Steinberg dithering.
- A "Cheer-ready" toggle appends a space-delimited `Cheer100` token plus a small
  visible rotating nonce so repeated payloads slip past duplicate-message filters.
